evals.prompt: I've finished the SOTA review. Please run gap analysis on Research/20260410_eeg-adhd-children/
evals.expected_output: step7_gap_analysis.md with 2-3 evidence-linked gaps, 3-axis scoring, bilingual content, abstract-only caveat (this session has 0 full texts), and Checkpoint 3 prompt
assertions: Output file step7_gap_analysis.md exists in the session folder
The file contains YAML frontmatter with session_id, topic, step: 7, and priority_weights
The file contains an abstract-only warning/caveat since full_text_papers is 0
The abstract-only caveat appears before the main title (# Gap Analysis)
At least 2 gaps are identified, each with a GAP_NNN identifier
Each gap has Supporting Evidence section citing specific citation keys from the SOTA review
Each gap has a Priority Score table with Severity, Novelty, and Feasibility scores (1-5)
Each gap has a computed composite score using × symbol (not x) in the arithmetic
A Priority Ranking table compares all gaps side by side
Content is bilingual (English + Traditional Chinese) in descriptions and section headings
The file ends with Checkpoint 3 instructions mentioning 'Lock GAP' and 'drop GAP'
step0_session_config.json is updated with current_step: 7

evals.prompt: 幫我分析 Research/20260410_llm-automated-program-repair/ 的研究缺口
evals.expected_output: step7_gap_analysis.md with gaps specific to LLM-based program repair, evidence citations, priority ranking, bilingual
assertions: Output file step7_gap_analysis.md exists in the session folder
The file contains YAML frontmatter with step: 7
The file does NOT contain an abstract-only caveat (this session has 5 full-text papers)
At least 2 gaps are identified with GAP_NNN identifiers
Each gap is classified into one of the 5 gap types (Methodological, Population, Measurement, Temporal, Integration)
Each gap has Supporting Evidence citing papers from the SOTA review by citation key
Each gap has Counter-Evidence section (even if noting no counter-evidence exists)
Each gap has a 'Why It Matters' section explaining significance
Composite scores are arithmetically correct (severity×0.40 + novelty×0.30 + feasibility×0.30)
Content is bilingual with Traditional Chinese translations
Checkpoint 3 prompt appears at the end

evals.prompt: I completed the SOTA for the session in Research/20260410_bci-depression-treatment/. Find the research gaps please.
evals.expected_output: step7_gap_analysis.md with gaps for EEG neurofeedback ADHD, evidence-linked, priority ranking, NO abstract-only caveat
assertions: Output file step7_gap_analysis.md exists in the session folder
The file does NOT contain an abstract-only caveat (this session has 5 full-text papers)
At least 2 gaps are identified with supporting evidence from papers in the SOTA review
Each gap has 3-axis scoring (Severity, Novelty, Feasibility) with scores between 1-5
A Gap Landscape Summary section exists covering Coverage Strength and Methodology Distribution
The Executive Summary provides an overview of the gap landscape
All gaps are ranked by composite score in a Priority Ranking table
Bilingual content throughout (English + Traditional Chinese)
Checkpoint 3 instructions present at the end
step0_session_config.json updated with current_step: 7
